projects.title: Projects shaped around impact, operations and experience.
projects.intro: Commercial software, internal tools and systems where we improved the whole workflow.
projects.featuredLabel: The thread across most of my work:
projects.featuredBody: clear interfaces, process automation and systems shaped by business reality.

items.title: Casa del Kumis - Website
items.description: Responsive website focused on brand presence, product catalog clarity and local SEO performance.

caseStudy.problem: The company needed a stronger digital presence with better product visibility and more credibility online.
caseStudy.solution: A responsive website with semantic structure, optimized media and clearer commercial storytelling.
caseStudy.impact: A more polished digital presence with improved mobile reading, stronger performance and better SEO foundations.

items.title: POS - Casa del Kumis
items.description: Point-of-sale software running across 18 branches with Credinbanco payment integration.

caseStudy.problem: Branches relied on manual records, had weak centralized visibility and lacked robust electronic payment flows.
caseStudy.solution: A React POS system with operational tooling and direct Credinbanco integration for card terminal payments.
caseStudy.impact: Production software used across all 18 branches with much more consistent day-to-day operations.

items.title: Bank Reconciliation Tool
items.description: A financial reconciliation tool that cross-checks vouchers against bank statements in minutes.

caseStudy.problem: Bank reconciliation was manual, slow and highly error-prone each accounting period.
caseStudy.solution: A web tool that automates the matching between Credinbanco vouchers and bank statements.
caseStudy.impact: The process dropped from hours to minutes and made operational auditing much easier.

items.title: ERP - Food Sales System
items.description: Java MVC ERP for sales, clients, inventory and reporting in businesses with daily operational pressure.

caseStudy.problem: Small distribution businesses lacked an integrated system for sales, inventory and customer management.
caseStudy.solution: A desktop ERP built with Java MVC, JDBC/MySQL persistence and Excel export support.
caseStudy.impact: A functional operational base with core modules already connected for commercial management.

items.title: Facturas App - Invoice OCR
items.description: Thesis backend that processes invoice images and extracts key fields using OCR, computer vision and FastAPI.

caseStudy.problem: Manual invoice processing was slow, repetitive and difficult to scale for higher volumes.
caseStudy.solution: A Python pipeline to preprocess images, detect text and extract NIT, date, total and line items.
caseStudy.impact: Automation with accuracy above 94 percent on key extracted fields.

items.title: Personal Portfolio
items.description: This portfolio built with Next.js 15, i18n, Framer Motion, Tailwind v4 and a Resend-powered contact flow.

caseStudy.problem: I needed a modern bilingual presence that reflected both technical depth and visual judgment.
caseStudy.solution: A portfolio with App Router, next-intl, stronger interaction design and clearer storytelling around work and capability.
caseStudy.impact: A site that can present work, start conversations and communicate value far more effectively.

experience.title: Real operating context, not isolated demo work.
experience.intro: Software that affects sales, operations and visibility. That changes how we decide to build.

bullets: ERP sales + inventory with Java MVC
Desktop UI with Swing and Excel export

tools.title: Technology selected with judgment, not trend chasing.
tools.intro: Frontend, backend, data and deployment. Stack chosen for the problem, goal is constant: clarity and speed.
capabilities: Frontend shaped for rhythm, hierarchy and serious responsive behavior.
Backend for workflows, integrations and operational automation.
End-to-end delivery from architecture through deployment.

contact.title: If there is an idea, product or stuck system, we can move it forward.
contact.subtitle: I work best when a product needs structure, a digital experience needs sharper UX or an internal flow needs to stop wasting time.
contact.responseBody: Quick response, concrete problem, clear direction before code.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers about timing, collaboration style and the kinds of projects where I am most useful.

contactPage.title: Let us talk with context and direction.
contactPage.subtitle: If you have an idea, a system that needs improvement or a product experience that feels weak, we can turn it into something clearer, stronger and better executed.
highlights: **Reply within 24 to 48h** with a clearer direction
**Product perspective** to understand the business and not only the stack
**Iterative delivery** so progress becomes visible early
